message:
✨ In-cluster agent banner with setup dialog (#2310)

* ✨ Add in-cluster agent banner with setup dialog

When the console is running in a Kubernetes cluster (Helm install) and
no kc-agent connection is detected, show a blue banner prompting the
user to either install the agent or configure CORS. Clicking the banner
opens a modal with two paths:

1. Install the kc-agent (brew install or build from source)
2. Already have an agent? Configure CORS with KC_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
   set to the current page origin (dynamically computed)

The banner uses the same stacking system as existing demo/offline
banners and is positioned between the demo and offline banners.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Sync global demoMode singleton when auth enables demo

When auth.tsx's setDemoMode() sets the demo token, it now also calls
the global setDemoMode(true) from lib/demoMode.ts. Without this sync,
the in-cluster banner never rendered because:

1. Auth sets token=demo-token (user state only)
2. demoMode singleton stays false (initialized before auth ran)
3. Layout's auto-demo-enable skips when isInClusterMode
4. isDemoMode stays false → no banner

This ensures the useDemoMode() hook immediately reflects the demo
state set by auth, regardless of agent or cluster mode.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,278 @@ +'use client' + +import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react' +import { Plug, Copy, Check, ChevronDown, ChevronRight, Terminal, Shield, ExternalLink } from 'lucide-react' +import { BaseModal } from '../../lib/modals' +import { UI_FEEDBACK_TIMEOUT_MS } from '../../lib/constants/network' + +interface InClusterAgentDialogProps { + isOpen: boolean + onClose: () => void +} + +const BREW_INSTALL_CMD = 'brew tap kubestellar/tap && brew install --head kc-agent && kc-agent' +const BUILD_FROM_SOURCE_CMD = 'git clone https://github.com/kubestellar/console.git && cd console && go build -o bin/kc-agent ./cmd/kc-agent && ./bin/kc-agent' +const DOCS_URL = 'https://console-docs.kubestellar.io' + +/** Step key ranges: 100–199 = install section, 200–299 = CORS section */ +const COPY_KEY_BREW = 100 +const COPY_KEY_BUILD = 101 +const COPY_KEY_CORS_ENV = 200 +const COPY_KEY_CORS_FLAG = 201 + +export function InClusterAgentDialog({ isOpen, onClose }: InClusterAgentDialogProps) { + const [copiedStep, setCopiedStep] = useState(null) + const [showBuildFromSource, setShowBuildFromSource] = useState(false) + const [showCorsDetails, setShowCorsDetails] = useState(false) + const copiedTimerRef = useRef>() + + useEffect(() => { + return () => clearTimeout(copiedTimerRef.current) + }, []) + + const currentOrigin = typeof window !== 'undefined' ? window.location.origin : 'https://your-console-url' + + const corsEnvCmd = `KC_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=${currentOrigin} kc-agent` + const corsFlag = `kc-agent -allowed-origins ${currentOrigin}` + + const copyToClipboard = async (text: string, stepKey: number) => { + await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text) + setCopiedStep(stepKey) + clearTimeout(copiedTimerRef.current) + copiedTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCopiedStep(null), UI_FEEDBACK_TIMEOUT_MS) + } + + return ( + + + + +
+ {/* Architecture note */} +
+
+
+ i +
+
+

+ The console is running in-cluster but + no kc-agent connection was detected. The agent bridges your browser to your Kubernetes clusters: +

+
+ Browser + {' \u2192 '} + Console (in-cluster) + {' \u2192 '} + kc-agent + {' \u2192 '} + Your clusters +
+
+
+
+ + {/* Section 1: Install the agent */} +
+
+
+ +
+
+
+ Option 1: Install the kc-agent +
+

+ Run the agent on a machine that has access to your kubeconfig +

+ + {/* Brew install */} +
+ + {BREW_INSTALL_CMD} + + +
+ + {/* Build from source (collapsible) */} +
+ + {showBuildFromSource && ( +
+
+ + {BUILD_FROM_SOURCE_CMD} + + +
+
+ )} +
+ +

+ The agent reads your kubeconfig and streams live cluster data to this console. +

+
+
+
+ + {/* Section 2: Configure CORS */} +
+
+
+ +
+
+
+ Option 2: Already have an agent? +
+

+ If you already installed the kc-agent but the console can't connect, your agent + likely needs to allow this origin in its CORS configuration. +

+ + {/* CORS env var */} +

+ Set the KC_ALLOWED_ORIGINS environment variable: +

+
+ + {corsEnvCmd} + + +
+ + {/* Or CLI flag */} +

+ Or use the CLI flag: +

+
+ + {corsFlag} + + +
+ + {/* CORS details (collapsible) */} +
+ + {showCorsDetails && ( +
+

+ When the console runs in a cluster, your browser connects directly to the kc-agent + via HTTP/WebSocket. Browsers block these cross-origin requests unless the agent + explicitly allows the console's origin. +

+

+ By default, the agent allows connections from localhost,{' '} + 127.0.0.1, and{' '} + console.kubestellar.io. + For custom domains or ingress URLs, add your origin with{' '} + KC_ALLOWED_ORIGINS. +

+

+ Multiple origins can be comma-separated:{' '} + KC_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://a.example.com,https://b.example.com +

+
+ )} +
+
+
+
+
+ +
+ + + Documentation + +
+ + + +
+ Current origin: {currentOrigin} +
+
+ + + + ) +}

+    // Sync the global demoMode singleton so Layout banners and useDemoMode() hook

   }, [])
